reasoning: The market price on Feb 19th was 31%. At that time, we now know Devin Booker had just suffered a hip strain that would sideline him for four games. Despite this, the Suns managed to stay competitive and significantly, they beat the division-leading Lakers 113-110 in a head-to-head matchup on Feb 26th. Before that game, the Lakers held a 1.5-game lead in the division; the Suns' victory directly shrinks that gap to 0.5 games. Furthermore, Devin Booker has returned to 5-on-5 practice as of March 1 and is expected to play in the next game, mitigating the injury risk that was present when the market priced them at 31%. Although the Lakers appear to be a powerhouse (having somehow acquired Luka Doncic), the standings are extremely close with a month left. Trailing by only 0.5 games with their star returning, the Suns' chances of winning the division have materially improved since the Feb 19th price. I estimate their probability around 42%.
